title: Minimalist Modern Tall Dresser
summary: A tall, narrow-drawer dresser built mostly from ripped plywood strips and 1x trim, with five drawers and beveled shadow-gap faces.
description:
This dresser is built for accessories and smaller items, not bulk clothing storage — it trades drawer depth for height, and five narrow drawers instead of the usual four or five wide ones.

Most of the case is **ripped-down plywood strips rather than full panels**, which is what gives it a minimal, seamless look instead of the visible face-frame-and-panel look of a traditional dresser. Ripping the plywood first, then crosscutting to length, keeps every strip a consistent width without needing to rip each piece individually later.

The drawer faces get an inside 45° bevel along the top edge, which creates a subtle shadow gap between drawers instead of a flat butt joint — a small detail that reads as considered rather than accidental.

tools.note: Rips the plywood sheets and 1x stock down to width.
Checks each frame for square via diagonal measurements.

materials.name: 3/4" plywood, 4x8 sheet
materials.note: Ripped into 16in wide strips for the sides and top.

materials.name: 1/4" plywood, half sheet (4x4 ft)
materials.note: Ripped into 16in wide strips for drawer bottoms.

materials.name: Wood screws, 1-1/4"
materials.note: Reinforces the drawer faces from behind.

cutList.note: Ripped to 8-1/2in wide with an inside 45-degree bevel on the top edge.

steps.title: Rip and crosscut all stock
steps.body: Rip the plywood sheets into 16in wide strips, then crosscut them to the side-panel and top-panel sizes. Rip the 1x stock to width, cutting the longest pieces first.

steps.title: Build the front frame
steps.body: Build the front frame from 1x3 strips with brad nails and glue, checking it for square by comparing its two diagonal measurements.

steps.title: Assemble the case sides
steps.body: Attach the remaining 1x3 supports to the two plywood side panels — mirrored left and right — then attach the front frame to the assembled sides.

steps.title: Trim the base and build the top
steps.body: Attach 1x2 trim around the bottom of the case. Attach trim to the top panel's ends and front, glue it in place on the case, and nail through every available attachment point.

steps.title: Trim the front and back edges
steps.body: Attach trim to the front and back edges of the case, flush to the outside faces.

steps.title: Build and hang the drawers
steps.body: Build the five drawer boxes from 1x6 stock and install them on euro-style slides. Rip the 1x10 drawer faces to width with an inside 45-degree bevel on the top edge, attach them to the drawer boxes with brad nails, then reinforce each from behind with screws.
